features.description: Pin the iOS app to a light appearance via .preferredColorScheme(.light) at the RootView/WindowGroup root, so every screen, field, and SwiftUI Form renders in the intended light-paper palette regardless of the system Dark Mode setting. This fixes the white-on-white field text (TextField/SecureField previously used SwiftUI's adaptive .primary, which turned white in Dark Mode over the fixed light card backgrounds) and the inverse dark-Form-background problem in one change. Additionally set explicit .foregroundStyle(Theme.ink) on the field text as belt-and-suspenders.
features.notes: Verification: a new XCUITest launches the app under a forced Dark Mode simulator appearance (simctl ui <udid> appearance dark) and asserts the auth fields and add-task field remain present/interactive; existing 20 iOS tests (15 unit + 5 UI) still pass; manual visual sign-off that fields render dark-ink on light. No web files change. Theme.ink (0x333333) already exists (Theme.swift:8), so Theme.swift may need no edit (scope superset).
approaches_tried: First attempt used an -AppleInterfaceStyle Dark launch argument to force the app dark in the UI test — this does NOT force dark on the iOS 26.5 simulator, producing a false-green (test passed even with the pin removed). Replaced with running the test on a simulator set to Dark appearance via 'xcrun simctl ui <sim> appearance dark', which gives a genuine red (pin off) / green (pin on).
Fix that worked: .preferredColorScheme(.light) at the WindowGroup root (propagates to sheets) + explicit .foregroundStyle(Theme.ink) on all fields as belt-and-suspenders.

features.description: Add an iOS app icon (Warm Editorial sunrise: terracotta half-sun rising over a sage horizon on a warm dawn gradient), 1024x1024 opaque, wired via Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset.
features.coverage: n/a (asset; verified by build compiling AppIcon into Assets.car + Info.plist CFBundleIcons)
features.notes: Icon designed as HTML rendered at 1024 via Playwright, flattened opaque. Xcode auto-generates all sizes from the single 1024 source. Installed and verified on the XR.
approaches_tried: Rendered icon design via headless browser at exact 1024x1024; first composition had rays hidden behind the horizon band, fixed layering (rays behind sun, ground clips sun bottom).

features.description: On-device data layer (foundational): add LocalStore conforming to PlannerApi, backed by SwiftData, and wire PlannerStore to use it on iOS instead of the cloud ApiClient. Auth is local: me() returns a fixed User(id:'local',email:'',name:'You'); signIn/signUp/signOut are no-ops. @Model entities: TaskEntity (mirrors PlannerTask + completedAt:Date?, updatedAt:Date, auditRecordId:UUID?), EventEntity, CaptureItem(id,text,source[typed|share],status[pending|classified|filed|reviewed],createdAt), ReviewItem(id,captureId,cleanedTitle,suggestedBucket,suggestedDay,suggestedStart:Int?,suggestedMinutes:Int?,confidence:Double,auditRecordId:UUID,createdAt), AuditRecord(id,captureId,rawInput,chosenBucket,confidence:Double,autoFiled:Bool,modelTier[foundationModels|ruleBased],createdAt,correctionsJSON:String). Device starts empty (no cloud migration; sync deferred to F009). Keep existing mock-based Store tests; add SwiftData CRUD/persistence tests. All planner flows work offline; data persists across relaunch; coverage >=95%.
features.notes: Local-first pivot: iOS persists to SwiftData, opens straight to the planner (no auth). UI tests use a UITEST_RESET launch flag for isolation.
approaches_tried: SwiftData #Predicate with captured vars crashes at fetch -> fetch-all-and-filter in Swift.
Many ModelContainers/process crashes -> one shared in-memory container per test class, wiped per test.
Capturing container.mainContext at construction then using it later traps (Core Data thread confinement) -> PlannerApi @MainActor + access mainContext lazily inside methods.
Deleted the dead cloud ApiClient (LocalStore replaces it) and AuthView (no login in local-first) rather than carry uncovered dead code.

features.description: AI quick-capture (natural language -> bucketed, scheduled task): CaptureClassifier protocol returning {bucket,day,startMin?,durationMin?,cleanedTitle,confidence}. Two tiers: FoundationModels (@Generable, iOS 26+) and a deterministic RuleBased fallback (iOS<26 / unavailable). RuleBased: whole-token case-insensitive keyword map [urgent/progress/extra], score=count of matches, confidence=clamp(0.30..0.95, 0.5+0.45*(top-second)/max(top,1)), zero matches->extra@0.40, ties broken by priority urgent>progress>extra. Date/time parser (device-local): today/tomorrow/tonight/'in N days'/bare weekday=next future/'next <weekday>'=+7d/bare times/durations/no-time->unscheduled. A capture becomes a TaskEntity in the chosen bucket, scheduled if timed. FoundationModels tested for decode+range+fallback (not exact values); classifier injectable. Coverage >=95%.
features.coverage: new classifier files 90-97% (reachable logic 100%; misses are Swift `??` autoclosure artifacts + iOS<26 availability branches); capture wiring Store 98.88% / PlannerView 99.52%
features.notes: Bucket output is a deterministic total function (tie-break urgent>progress>extra); spec gate PASS.
approaches_tried: Two-tier classifier behind CaptureClassifier protocol: RuleBased (deterministic, 16 oracle tests) + FoundationModels @Generable (iOS 26+), unified by CaptureEngine which validates FM output (isInRange) and falls back on throw/out-of-range.
Extracted CaptureGuess.asClassification() as a pure function so model-output normalization is unit-tested independently of the non-deterministic model call.
On-device model is unavailable on the simulator (Model Catalog error) — CaptureEngine`s try? fallback keeps capture working; verified end-to-end via UI test.

features.description: Confidence-gated auto-filing (Bouncer) + review queue: threshold default 0.6 (0.3-0.9, Settings/UserDefaults). Every classification writes one AuditRecord at classification time. confidence>=threshold: create TaskEntity (autoFiled=true) with auditRecordId set. confidence<threshold: create ReviewItem (auditRecordId set), NO task. Review UI: Accept creates the task from suggested (possibly edited) values; Dismiss discards; un-actioned ReviewItems persist. Unit-tested with a stub classifier emitting known confidences. Coverage >=95%.
features.coverage: Bouncer 100% / Api 100% / LocalStore 97.6% / Store 98.6% / PlannerView 99.6% / EditSheets 96.3%
approaches_tried: One transactional LocalStore.fileCapture per capture: writes an AuditRecord, then either auto-files a TaskEntity (autoFiled, auditRecordId) or queues a ReviewItem — returns .filed/.queued so the UI knows which happened.
Pure Bouncer.autoFiles gate + CaptureThreshold (UserDefaults, clamped 0.3-0.9, default 0.6) unit-tested in isolation.
Added tier to Classification so the AuditRecord records which classifier fired.
Injected UserDefaults into PlannerStore so capture-flow tests are hermetic (the shared simulator threshold domain leaked between the settings UI test and the unit capture tests otherwise).

features.description: Daily digest (top-3 + one stuck + one small win): pure DigestService.digest(tasks:events:today:)->Digest. Top-3 from INCOMPLETE tasks only, ordered (overdue<today<future) then (urgent<progress<extra) then position then id. stuck = at most one incomplete task with day<today (oldest). small win = at most one task with completedAt within last 3 days (most recent). todayEvents = events with day==today (unranked). nil stuck/win allowed. Deterministic; unit-tested over seeded data. Coverage >=95%.
features.coverage: DigestService 98.5% (one miss is the unreachable invalid-date guard); surfaced via PlannerView DigestCard, wired in Store/LocalStore, all >95%
approaches_tried: Pure DigestService.digest(tasks:events:today:) using tuple sort keys: top3 by (dayCategory, bucketRank, position, id); stuck = oldest overdue incomplete; smallWin = most recent completion within 3 days of midnight today; todayEvents unranked.
Added completedAt to the PlannerTask DTO (default nil so the memberwise init stays source-compatible) so the digest can compute recent wins.

Exposed digest(today:) as a computed data-layer query (like day()/earlier()) since the digest spans all days, not just the loaded one; PlannerStore recomputes it on load and a DigestCard surfaces it on the Today view.

features.description: Correction / audit loop: each classification writes exactly one immutable AuditRecord (captureId,rawInput,chosenBucket,confidence,autoFiled,modelTier,createdAt). A correction = a change to bucket OR schedule(day/scheduledStart) on a task with auditRecordId!=nil, appended {field,old,new,at} to correctionsJSON; title/note edits excluded; records append-only. History view lists records. Learning/behavior-change out of scope (signal capture only). Unit-tested. Coverage >=95%.
features.coverage: Audit 100% / LocalStore 95.5%+ (correction logging + auditHistory); History UI covered by UI test; all >95%
approaches_tried: Correction detection lives in LocalStore.patchTask: before applying the patch, for a task with auditRecordId!=nil it appends {field,old,new,at} to the linked AuditRecord.correctionsJSON for each of bucket/day/scheduledStart that actually changes (title/note excluded; no-op changes skipped; append-only).
CorrectionLog (JSON, ISO8601 dates) serializes the log; AuditEntry is the UI-facing view; auditHistory() sorts records newest-first.
History sheet (menu) lists each capture with its filing decision and corrections; verified end-to-end by scheduling an audited capture and reading it back from History.

features.description: Home-screen Widget + Share extension capture: App Group shared SwiftData container. Widget deep-links to the capture field (no in-widget entry; widget captures are source=typed). Share extension writes a raw CaptureItem(status=pending, source=share) and returns without classifying; the main app classifies pending captures via the F004/F005 pipeline on next launch. Coverage applies to shared classify/capture logic, not the extension UI shell.

Device <-> web sync: let the iOS local-first data optionally sync with the cloud web backend (map a device to a web account, push/pull tasks/events, resolve conflicts). NEEDS SPECIFICATION before implementation — deliberately not spec'd yet. Deferred epic that unblocks cross-device continuity between the iOS (local) and web (cloud) versions.
features.notes: TRACKED, NEEDS SPECIFICATION. Do not implement until spec'd via the spec gate. Depends conceptually on F003 (local store) + the web backend.
